title: Pit-wave field pressure features
summary: F1 stops cluster around strategy windows; once comparable cars pit, competitors often respond to undercut/overcut pressure.
rationale: F1 stops cluster around strategy windows; once comparable cars pit, competitors often respond to undercut/overcut pressure. Current best only adds one rival-ahead tyre feature, not whole-field pit-wave state.
implementation_hint: Within each Race/LapNumber, derive causal features from PitStop distribution: count/share of cars with PitStop incremented or high PitStop, cumulative stopped share by stint, same-compound stopped share, and lagged lap pit counts. Add interactions with Position, TyreLife, and RaceProgress.
expected_effect: May improve AUC by capturing synchronized stop windows that tyre age alone misses.
risk: If PitStop semantics are cumulative rather than per-lap, increment detection must be grouped by Race/Driver and sorted by LapNumber; careless use of future laps would leak.
